scope: Single-cell meta-analysis platform for IBD across fetal, pediatric, and adult stages; blood, small intestine, and large intestine; healthy, inflamed UC, and inflamed CD states.
included_diseases: ulcerative colitis; Crohn disease; healthy/control states
approx_size: Approximately 1.14 million single cells from 12 datasets; 9 major cell classes and 101 cell subtypes
publication_title: Single-cell meta-analysis of inflammatory bowel disease with scIBD
limitations: Integrated atlas, not a primary cohort. Source-dataset composition and harmonization choices should be reviewed before using for formal meta-analysis.

resource_name: Gut Cell Atlas / Human Gut Cell Atlas
resource_type: Pan-GI integrated single-cell and spatial atlas
scope: Integrated human gastrointestinal atlas including healthy and disease datasets; includes UC and CD disease projections.
included_diseases: ulcerative colitis; Crohn disease; celiac disease; GI cancers; healthy/control tissue
approx_size: Approximately 1.6 million cells across integrated gut datasets
source_datasets: Multiple GI single-cell and spatial datasets; reported as 25+ datasets in public summaries
publication_title: Single-cell integration reveals metaplasia in inflammatory gut diseases
limitations: Broader pan-GI resource rather than IBD-only. Disease projections and source cohorts should be interpreted in the context of the atlas integration model.

scope: Public CELLxGENE collections containing IBD-relevant single-cell datasets and standardized cell/tissue/disease annotations.
omics_layers: Single-cell transcriptomics; Spatial transcriptomics where available
included_diseases: ulcerative colitis; Crohn disease; controls across matching collections
source_datasets: Multiple CELLxGENE collections; should be split into collection-backed rows for formal analysis
limitations: Portal-level row. Each collection has its own source publication, sample count, and annotation model.

scope: HCA intestinal and IBD-related single-cell projects, including specific project-backed datasets that may also appear in GEO or CELLxGENE.
omics_layers: Single-cell transcriptomics; Spatial transcriptomics; Multi-omics where available
included_diseases: Crohn disease; controls; broader gut atlas references
source_datasets: Multiple HCA projects; primary project-backed rows remain in the Dataset Explorer when accession-specific
access_model: Public portal; some registration or controlled components possible
limitations: Consortium portal rather than one dataset. Individual HCA projects should be represented as primary rows when accession-specific.

scope: Manually curated IBD bulk transcriptomics database with differential-expression comparisons and exploration tools.
included_diseases: IBD; Crohn disease; ulcerative colitis; controls across curated studies
approx_size: 2,932 samples across 34 curated bulk transcriptomic datasets
access_model: Public Shiny portal and GitHub repository
publication_title: IBDTransDB: a manually curated transcriptomic database for inflammatory bowel disease
limitations: Aggregated resource; individual dataset metadata and harmonization choices need review for formal reuse.

resource_type: IBD clinical and biospecimen consortium resource
scope: Crohn?s & Colitis Foundation research platform linking IBD biospecimens, clinical data, patient-reported outcomes, and molecular data.
approx_size: Large consortium resource; exact accessible counts depend on study/program
source_datasets: SPARC IBD and related IBD Plexus programs/resources
limitations: Not a simple public-download dataset. Access and data availability depend on the program, proposal, and governance route.

scope: International consortium resource for IBD, CD, and UC genetic studies and summary-statistic resources.
approx_size: Resource/publication dependent; broad publication reported 96,486 individuals; OpenGWAS resource reported 65,642 for one summary dataset
source_datasets: Multiple GWAS cohorts and consortium datasets
access_model: Public processed summary statistics; controlled access for individual-level genotype data
publication_title: Association analyses identify 38 susceptibility loci for inflammatory bowel disease and highlight shared genetic risk across populations
limitations: Consortium/resource-level entry; exact ancestry, phenotype, and accession depend on selected subresource.

scope: EMBL-EBI GWAS Catalog trait pages and summary-statistics links for IBD, CD, and UC.
limitations: Trait-level aggregate; one DOI, PMID, and sample size are not applicable.

scope: Integrated public genetics, locus-to-gene, colocalization, and target-prioritization evidence for IBD-related traits and targets.
included_diseases: IBD; Crohn disease; ulcerative colitis queryable traits/resources
source_datasets: Integrated public GWAS and functional genomics sources
access_model: Public portal and downloads/API for processed/integrated results
publication_title: Open Targets Genetics: systematic identification of trait-associated genes using large-scale genetics and functional genomics
limitations: Not a primary dataset; conclusions depend on source evidence and scoring model.

resource_name: FinnGen / UK Biobank IBD GWAS Resources
scope: Large population-biobank GWAS resources with IBD, CD, and UC endpoints/traits.
included_diseases: IBD; Crohn disease; ulcerative colitis phenotypes vary by resource
approx_size: FinnGen R10 K11_IBD_STRICT reported 9,083 cases after genotype QC; UK Biobank resource counts vary by endpoint and ancestry
source_datasets: FinnGen; UK Biobank; OpenGWAS/Pan-UKB-style processed resources
access_model: Public processed summary/statistical resources; controlled access for individual-level biobank data
limitations: Combined row should eventually be split into FinnGen-specific and UK Biobank-specific rows for clean sample/accession metadata.
